name: NetTerm
description: SSH/telnet/serial/shell terminal for M5CardputerZero — profiles, OS-managed VPN, session logs, file injection (charset auto-detect), quick-send macros, Japanese input, font-size, EN/JA UI

store.title: NetTerm
store.summary: SSH / telnet / USB-serial / shell terminal with profiles, VPN, macros and file injection.
store.description: A pocket terminal for network gear: SSH, telnet, USB-serial console (picocom) and local shell. Connection profiles with OS-managed VPN, per-profile SSH key and color theme, session logging with search, quick-send macros, charset-auto-detect file injection with wait-for-prompt, scrollback with search and line copy/paste, full SGR colour, and a switchable EN/JA UI.

en.title: NetTerm
en.summary: SSH / telnet / USB-serial / shell terminal with profiles, VPN, macros and file injection.

ja.title: NetTerm
ja.summary: SSH / telnet / USB-シリアル / シェル端末。プロファイル・VPN・マクロ・ファイル流し込み対応。

privacy.data_leaves_device: only to the remote hosts the user chooses to connect to (ssh/telnet/serial); no telemetry
privacy.data_retention: connection profiles and session logs are stored locally on /sdcard until the user deletes them

risk_flags: privileged VPN bring-up via pkexec (wg-quick/nmcli etc.)
arbitrary network egress as an ssh/telnet client

review.notes: Built and verified in an arm64 Debian Bookworm container: the app runs, seeds its config and exits cleanly on SIGTERM, and the .deb passes the packages/ validate-pr.yml checks. NOT yet verified on physical M5CardputerZero hardware — the evdev keyboard's Fn/symbol layer and SIDE keycode plus the fbdev display path are implemented against standard Linux keycodes and await on-device keymap confirmation. Disclosed up front per the honest-metadata policy. Source is public (open-source, MIT).
